steps.title: Attach the absolute phrase
steps.content: The sentence first names two civil rights events. The words after the blank, “the former establishing…” and “the latter legally affirming…,” form an absolute phrase that explains what each event did.

steps.title: Reject fragments and run-ons
steps.content: A adds “as,” which would require finite verbs rather than participles. B turns the explanatory phrase into a fragment. C gives no punctuation between the main clause and the phrase. A comma attaches the explanatory absolute phrase correctly. The answer is D.
